article_title: Warp Open-Sources Its Agentic Terminal Under AGPLv3 With OpenAI as Founding Sponsor and an Agent-First Contribution Model
summary: Warp open-sources its agentic terminal under AGPLv3 — all 8 snapshots verified and every named claim is supported, but the article puts a Kilo Blog paraphrase inside quote marks (the Kilo source uses different wording). One subordinate correction filed.

content_preview.title: Warp Open-Sources Its Agentic Terminal Under AGPLv3 With OpenAI as Founding Sponsor and an Agent-First Contribution Model
content_preview.summary: After four years of promises, Warp released its terminal client on GitHub under AGPLv3, with OpenAI sponsoring a contribution workflow that has agents writing code while humans focus on review.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Warp, the Rust-based AI terminal that has been promising to go open source for years, finally released its client codebase on GitHub on April 28, 2026, [according to the company's announcement](https://www.warp.dev/blog/warp-is-now-open-source). The release places the bulk of the client under AGPLv3 while the UI framework crates ship under MIT, and it positions OpenAI as the repository's founding sponsor in a contribution workflow that explicitly asks AI agents — not humans — to do ...

recommendations: Add warp.dev, fossforce.com, byteiota.com, blog.kilo.ai to config/source_allowlist.txt. news.ycombinator.com is intentionally not in allowlist (user-generated content); leave that warning standing as a soft reminder when HN threads are cited.

editor_notes.content_quality: Long-form News piece (999 words) with clear structure: Overview / What Was Released / What Did Not Open / Agent-First Contribution Model / Reception and Loose Ends / What We Don't Know. Strong attribution density, every claim has an inline link.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 8 snapshots fetched at HTTP 200 and grep-verified per-claim. source-0 (warp.dev): confirms April 28 launch, AGPL-3.0, warpui_core/warpui MIT split, Kimi/MiniMax/Qwen, 'auto (open)' routing, founding-sponsor language, Sottiaux quote verbatim, 'agents doing the heavy lifting' phrasing verbatim. source-1 (github.com/warpdotdev/warp): confirms repository description 'an agentic development environment, born out of the terminal', Rust, AGPL, and current star count ~57.8k (article says 'above 57,000' — accurate). source-2 (It's FOSS): confirms warpui MIT/AGPL split, all model names, Zach Lloyd quote verbatim 'Open-sourcing is fundamentally coming from our desire to build a successful business…'. source-3 (Hacker News thread): confirms Lloyd verbatim 'I actually think we can build a better product, more quickly if we build it with our community + agents', the 'I was very surprised to see they chose my favorite free software license — the AGPLv3' verbatim, shimman 'tweaking an Alacritty fork then using that to get a \$50million venture round then giving zero money' verbatim, nixpulvis 'absolutely no hard feelings' verbatim. source-4 (FOSS Force): confirms 'flirting — and not flirting — with making Warp open source for about four years' verbatim, Lloyd 'biggest bottleneck to development is no longer writing code — it's all the human-in-the-loop activities around the code' verbatim, Warp Drive / Oz closed-and-commercial framing. source-5 (Help Net Security): confirms April 30 date, Kimi/MiniMax/Qwen, founding sponsor framing, Oz/OpenAI GPT models. source-6 (byteIOTA): confirms 37,000+ stars, #2 GitHub trending, 700,000+ developers, OpenWarp fork. source-7 (Kilo Blog): confirms BYOK for Anthropic/Google/OpenAI on paid plans, OpenWarp fork — BUT the article puts inside quote marks 'AI features depend on Warp's backend services, preventing fully self-hosted experiences' while Kilo's actual text is 'AI features still depend on Warp's backend services, so even if you build from source, you're not getting a fully self-hosted experience.' This is a paraphrase rendered as a verbatim direct quote — exactly the case the v3.9.0 policy says use APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS for.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every named entity, statistic, and date traces to a verified snapshot. The only attribution issue is the Kilo Blog 'AI features depend on…' paraphrase rendered as a direct quote. The substance is accurate; only the framing as a verbatim quote is wrong.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral throughout. The auto-script's 'loaded language' warning for 'absolutely' triggers on the nixpulvis Hacker News quote ('absolutely no hard feelings') — that's a verbatim user quote, not editorial voice. Not a real concern.
editor_notes.originality: Distinct topic. Internal links to 2026-04-19 Cursor 3 article and 2026-04-29 Intel OSS retreat article both verified to exist. No prior Warp coverage in 2026-05.
concerns: Article puts a paraphrase from Kilo Blog inside quote marks. Source says 'AI features still depend on Warp's backend services, so even if you build from source, you're not getting a fully self-hosted experience.' Article says: '"AI features depend on Warp's backend services, preventing fully self-hosted experiences"'. Substance matches, but quote marks should be removed or the wording corrected to match the source verbatim.
recommendations: On future articles, only use quote marks around verbatim source text. Paraphrases must be unquoted.

source_verification_per_url.notes: Confirms April 28 launch, AGPL-3.0, warpui MIT/AGPL split, Kimi/MiniMax/Qwen, 'auto (open)', Sottiaux quote, 'agents doing the heavy lifting' phrasing.
Repository metadata: 'an agentic development environment, born out of the terminal', Rust, AGPL, ~57.8k stars at fetch time.
Confirms warpui MIT/AGPL split, model lineup, Zach Lloyd business-rationale quote verbatim.
Confirms Lloyd 'community + agents' quote, AGPLv3-favorite-license comment, shimman Alacritty critique, nixpulvis 'absolutely no hard feelings' response.
Confirms 'flirting — and not flirting — about four years' phrasing, Lloyd bottleneck quote, Warp Drive/Oz closed-and-commercial framing.
Confirms 'auto (open)', Oz cloud orchestration with GPT models, founding sponsor framing.
Confirms 37,000+ stars, #2 GitHub trending, 700,000+ developers, OpenWarp fork.

Confirms BYOK-on-paid-plans claim. BUT the 'AI features depend on Warp's backend services, preventing fully self-hosted experiences' inside quote marks in the article is a paraphrase; Kilo's actual text uses different wording (see corrections file).

editor_notes.verdict_decision_rationale: One subordinate paraphrase rendered as a direct quote in the body (not in headline, summary, or lead — which all hold up cleanly). The substance is accurate; only the punctuation framing is wrong. This is exactly the v3.9.0 APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS case: a single, isolated, recoverable issue that a correction note can honestly cover. The auto-script's allowlist warning and 'loaded language' false positive (from a verbatim user quote inside source-3) are not additional content defects.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Publish with one correction. Strong, well-sourced reporting on a notable open-source/agentic-development story.
